name: Ryuukyu Fireworks Festival
note: The earliest fireworks festival in Japan to welcome summer, combining Okinawan music and beach scenery.
venue: Ginowan Seaside Park Ginowan Tropical Beach
access: Take a temporary shuttle bus from Naha Bus Terminal or walk 5 minutes from the bus stop in front of the Convention Center.
rain: Held rain or shine; canceled in case of severe weather.

name: Lake Toya Long-Term Fireworks Festival (Daily)
note: From April 28 to October 31, fireworks are launched every night at 20:45 for 20 minutes. A recommended summer travel experience.
venue: Lake Toya Onsen Lakeside
access: About 20 minutes by Donan Bus from JR Toya Station to Lake Toya Onsen, then a 5-minute walk from the final stop.
rain: The event will proceed in the rain (canceled in strong winds).

name: Yokohama Port Opening Festival Fireworks
note: A flagship event in Kanto during June, featuring a collaboration of laser light shows and music.
venue: Inland Park, Yokohama
access: A 5-minute walk from Minato Mirai Station on the Minato Mirai Line, or a 15-minute walk from Sakuragicho Station on the JR Line.
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in severe weather.

name: Kyoto Art Fireworks
note: A precision music fireworks display featuring 30 minutes of synchronized fireworks with over 10,000 shots.
venue: JRA Kyoto Racecourse
access: Directly connected to Yodo Station on the Keihan Electric Railway, just a 2-minute walk.
rain: Held rain or shine; canceled in severe weather.

name: Adachi Fireworks Festival
note: The first large-scale fireworks event of the summer in Tokyo, featuring 15,000 fireworks launched in just one hour.
venue: Arakawa Riverbank (between Chiyoda Line Bridge and Nishiarai Bridge)
access: A 15-minute walk from JR, Tokyo Metro, or Tobu Railway's Kitasenju Station, or a 15-minute walk from Tobu Railway's Kosuge Station and Gotanno Station.
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather.

name: Atami Fireworks Festival (Summer Edition)
note: Held multiple times throughout the year, featuring a natural bass sound experience due to the basin and bay topography.
venue: Atami Bay (from Atami Port facilities to Atami Sun Beach)
access: A 15-minute walk from JR Atami Station.
rain: Held rain or shine (canceled in severe weather).

name: Sumida River Fireworks Festival
note: Last Saturday of July
venue: Venue 1: From Sakurabashi to Kototoibashi, Venue 2: From Komagata Bridge to Umayabashi
access: Venue 1: 15-minute walk from Tobu Railway and Tokyo Metro Asakusa Station, Venue 2: 5-minute walk from Toei Subway Oedo Line and Asakusa Line Kuramae Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather

name: Tenjin Matsuri Fireworks
note: Fixed date
venue: Kawasaki Park (next to the Mint Bureau) & Sakuranomiya Park
access: A 5-minute walk from Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line Tenmabashi Station or JR Tozai Line Osakajo-Kita Station.
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather

name: Bagoi Enshu Fireworks Festival
venue: Bagoi City Haranayagawa Riverside Park
access: A 20-minute walk from Aino Station or Bagoi Station on the JR Tōkaidō Line
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in severe weather

name: Abe River Fireworks Festival
venue: Abe River Riverside (near Tamachi 3-chome)
access: Approximately 40 minutes on foot from JR Shizuoka Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather (no postponement)

name: Toyota OIDEN Festival Fireworks
venue: Shirahama Park Area (Toyoda City, Aichi Prefecture)
access: 10-minute walk from Meitetsu Toyoda City Station / 10-minute walk from Aichi Loop Railway Shin-Toyota Station
rain: Held rain or shine; canceled in severe weather (no postponement)

name: Gion Kashiwazaki Fireworks Festival
venue: Kashiwazaki City Central Coast & Minato Town Seaside Park Area
access: 25-minute walk from JR Kashiwazaki Station
rain: Held in light rain; postponed in case of severe weather

name: Doshin-UHB Fireworks Festival
venue: Toyohira River Riverside (between Minami-Ohashi and Horohira Bridge)
access: Within walking distance from Toyosu Susukino Station, Gakuenmae Station, and Nakano Island Station.
rain: Postponed to August 7 in case of inclement weather.

name: Makuhari Beach Fireworks Festival
venue: Makuhari Seaside Park (Mihama Ward, Chiba City)
access: A 15 to 20-minute walk from JR Keiyo Line's Kaihin-Makuhari Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather or strong winds (no postponement)

name: Edogawa Fireworks Festival
note: First Saturday of August
venue: Edogawa Riverbank (in front of Shinozaki Park)
access: 15-minute walk from Toei Subway Shinozaki Station / 25-minute walk from JR Koiwa Station and Keisei Edogawa Station
rain: Cancelled in case of bad weather

name: Banqiao Fireworks Festival
note: The longest 700-meter Niagara Falls fireworks in Kanto
venue: Arakawa Riverbank (Itabashi, Tokyo)
count: 約15,000発（戸田橋花火大会と合算）
access: 20-minute walk from Toei Mita Line Hasune Station or Nishidai Station / 20-minute walk from JR Saikyo Line Ukima-Funado Station
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in severe weather (no postponement)

name: Okazaki Castle Summer Festival Fireworks
venue: Yoshikawa and Yahagigawa Riverbanks (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture)
access: 10-minute walk from Meitetsu Higashi-Okazaki Station / 10-minute walk from Meitetsu Okazaki Koen-mae Station / 10-minute walk from Aichi Loop Railway Naka-Okazaki Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather or river flooding (no postponement)

name: Kita-Kuni Grand Fireworks Festival
venue: Tedorigawa Riverside (Kawakita Town, Nomi District, Ishikawa Prefecture)
access: Approximately 15 minutes by shuttle bus or taxi from JR Nomi-Nakagami Station

name: Ashikaga Fireworks Festival
venue: Watarase River Tanaka Bridge Riverside (Ashikaga City, Tochigi Prefecture)
access: 5-minute walk from JR Ashikaga Station South Exit / 10-minute walk from Tobu Isesaki Line Ashikagashi Station
rain: Postponed to August 2nd in case of inclement weather

name: Koga Fireworks Festival
venue: Koga Golf Links (Watarase River Riverside)
access: 5-minute walk from Shin-Koga Station on the Tobu Nikko Line / 20-minute walk from Koga Station on the JR Tohoku Main Line
rain: Postponed to August 3 in case of inclement weather

name: Matsue Water Town Festival Fireworks
note: Held over two consecutive days
venue: East Shore of Lake Shinji (Matsue City, Suyoshi Area)
access: Approximately a 15-minute walk from JR Matsue Station
rain: Canceled in case of severe weather (no postponement)

name: Nagaoka Fireworks Festival
note: Fixed dates (extremely popular)
venue: Shinano River Riverside (Nagaoka City)
access: About a 30-minute walk from JR Nagaoka Station (shuttle buses available on the day)
rain: Held in light rain. Postponed in strong winds or flooding (8/2→8/16, 8/3→8/17)

name: Chikugo River Fireworks Festival
venue: Chikugo River Riverside (Kurume City, Kyomachi)
access: About a 10-minute walk from JR Kurume Station (Suitengu Exit)
rain: Postponed to the following day in case of rain

name: Sendai Tanabata Fireworks Festival
note: Pre-festival event on a fixed date
venue: Hirose River Riverside Area (near Nishi Park)
access: Approximately a 3-minute walk from Omachi Nishi Park Station on the Tozai Subway Line / About a 30-minute walk from Sendai Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather

name: Lake Biwa Grand Fireworks Festival
venue: Offshore area of Otsu Port (Hama-Otsu, Otsu City)
access: About a 5-minute walk from Keihan Electric Railway Biwako Hama-Otsu Station / About a 15-minute walk from JR Otsu Station
rain: Cancelled in case of inclement weather (no postponement)

name: Aomori Fireworks Festival
venue: Aomori Port (Aoi Umi Park)
access: About a 10-minute walk from the east exit of JR Aomori Station or Aoi Mori Railway Aomori Station.
rain: In case of severe weather, the event will be postponed to the next day (August 8).

name: Nagara River Fireworks Festival
venue: Along the Nagara River (between Nagara Bridge and Kinkazan Bridge)
access: About 20 minutes by special bus from JR Gifu Station
rain: The event will proceed in light rain. In case of severe weather or flooding, it will be postponed to August 22.

name: Tokachi Fireworks Festival
note: The start of the Bon Festival holiday
venue: Tokachi River Special Site (Obihiro City)
access: About a 20-minute walk from JR Obihiro Station (approximately 5 minutes by shuttle bus)
rain: In case of inclement weather, it will be postponed to the next day (8/14)

name: Kanmon Straits Fireworks Festival
note: Cross-prefectural (in competition with Fukuoka)
venue: Kanmon Straits (Shimonoseki side: Misaki Town Pier, Arukaport, Kamon Wharf)
access: 5 minutes by bus from JR Shimonoseki Station to 'Uminonakamichi' stop, then a 1-minute walk
rain: Held rain or shine; canceled in severe weather (no postponement)

name: Takamatsu Sanuki Fireworks Festival
venue: Around Sunport Takamatsu (Takamatsu City)
access: About a 5-minute walk from JR Takamatsu Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather

name: Fukuyama Summer Festival Ashida River Fireworks Festival
note: During the Bon Festival
venue: Ashida River Riverside (upstream of Ashida River Bridge, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture)
access: About 20 minutes by special bus from JR Fukuyama Station
rain: Postponed to August 17 in case of inclement weather

name: Akagawa Fireworks Festival
note: Voted among the most moving by Japanese fireworks enthusiasts, featuring a width of 700 meters for horizontal launches.
venue: Akagawa Riverside (between Haguro Bridge and Mikawa Bridge) (Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture)
access: Approximately 30 minutes on foot from JR Tsuruoka Station (no temporary buses available).
rain: Cancelled only in cases of safety concerns such as typhoons or thunderstorms (held even in light rain).

name: Suwa Lake Fireworks Festival
note: Fixed date (massive scale)
venue: Suwa Lakeside Park, Suwa City
access: About a 10-minute walk from JR Kamisuwa Station
rain: Cancelled in severe weather (proceeds in light rain)

name: Miyazu Lantern Floating Fireworks Festival
venue: Shimazaki Park and the surrounding area of Miyazu Bay (Miyazu City, Kyoto Prefecture)
access: Approximately a 10-minute walk from Miyazu Station on the Kyoto Tango Railway
rain: Not announced

name: Kumano Grand Fireworks Festival
venue: Shichiri Mihama Coast (Kumano City, Mie Prefecture)
access: Approximately a 5-minute walk from JR Kumano City Station
rain: Postponed to backup dates in case of bad weather (8/21, 8/24, 8/27)

name: National Fireworks Competition (Omagari Fireworks)
note: Last Saturday of August
venue: Omagari, Daisen City, along the Omono River (Akita Prefecture)
access: Approximately 30 minutes on foot from JR Omagari Station
rain: Postponement may occur in case of severe weather

name: Hokkaido Art Fireworks Festival
note: Autumn fireworks
venue: Moerenuma Park (Sapporo City, Hokkaido)
access: Take a bus from Toho Line's Kanjo-dori Higashi Station or Shindo-Higashi Station to Moerenuma Park stop.
rain: Event will proceed in light rain; canceled in severe weather.

name: Sakata Fireworks Festival
venue: Mogami River Riverside Park (Tsutsumicho, Sakata City, Yamagata Prefecture)
access: About 10 minutes by free shuttle bus from JR Sakata Station (or a 45-minute walk)
rain: Held rain or shine; canceled in case of severe weather; no postponements

name: Katakai Fireworks Festival
note: The world's largest single-shell firework, held over two consecutive days.
venue: Katakai Fireworks Launch Site, behind Asahara Shrine (Ojiya City, Niigata Prefecture)
access: Take a shuttle bus or taxi from JR Joetsu Line Ojiya Station.
rain: The event will proceed in the rain (rain gear is recommended).

name: Himeji Port Fireworks Festival
venue: Himeji Port (Shikama District) near Shikama Rinkai Bridge
access: Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Shikama Station on the Sanyo Electric Railway
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather (official announcement by 11 AM on the day)

name: Naniwa Yodogawa Fireworks Festival
note: Moved to autumn in 2026
venue: Yodogawa Riverside (from the Shingyodōsuji Yodogawa Bridge downstream to Route 2)
access: Approximately a 15-minute walk from Hankyu Jūsō Station / Approximately a 15-minute walk from Hanshin Himejima Station
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in severe weather; no postponement

name: Yatsushiro National Fireworks Competition
venue: Yatsushiro City Kumagawa River Green Space (upstream of Shin-Hagiwara Bridge)
access: Approximately a 10-minute walk from JR Yatsushiro Station or Higo-Orenji Railway Yatsushiro Station.
rain: The event will proceed in light rain; if severe weather occurs, it will be postponed to November 7.

name: Tamagawa Fireworks Festival
venue: Tamagawa Riverbank (between Futakotamagawa Bridge and the Third Keihin Road)
access: Approximately 20-30 minutes on foot from Tokyu Futakotamagawa Station (Setagaya) / About 15 minutes on foot from Futakoshinchi Station (Kawasaki)
rain: Held in rainy weather (umbrellas prohibited, raincoats recommended) - canceled in severe weather

name: Tokyo Bay Fireworks Festival
venue: Harumi Wharf and Harumi Park
access: Approximately a 15-minute walk from Toei Oedo Line Kachidoki Station
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather; no postponement

name: Tsuchiura All-Japan Fireworks Competition
note: One of the three major fireworks competitions
venue: Sakura Riverbank, Tsuchiura City (near Gakuen Ohashi)
access: About a 30-minute walk from Tsuchiura Station West Exit on the JR Joban Line (paid shuttle buses available)
rain: In case of inclement weather, it will be postponed to the following Saturday (11/14)

name: Chichibu Night Festival Fireworks Festival
note: Winter festival fireworks
venue: Yoshiyama Park (Launch Site) / Along National Route 140 (Viewing Area)
access: Approximately a 15-minute walk from Seibu Railway Seibu-Chichibu Station / Approximately a 5-minute walk from Chichibu Railway Chichibu Station
rain: Held in light rain; canceled in severe weather

name: SBI Fireworks Festival in Osaka Sennan
note: Summer fireworks on the southern side of Osaka Bay, held at the seaside of Izumisano City.
venue: SENNAN LONG PARK
access: Approximately a 10-minute walk from Tarui Station on the Nankai Main Line.
rain: Cancelled in case of severe weather.

name: Kobe Port Minato HANABI
note: Held over multiple nights in autumn at Meriken Park, Kobe Port.
venue: Meriken Park (fireworks launched from offshore platforms)
access: About a 10-minute walk from JR Motomachi Station East Exit / About an 8-minute walk from Exit 2 of Minatomotomachi Subway Station
rain: Held in rain; canceled in severe weather.

name: Ashiya Summer Carnival Fireworks
note: Ashiya Beach Park, late July summer festival
venue: Tide Ashiya Beach (Ashiya City, Hyogo Prefecture)
access: 30-minute walk from Hanshin Electric Railway Ashiya Station
rain: Proceeding in light rain; canceled in severe weather (no postponement)

name: Inagawa Fireworks Festival
note: Held along the Inagawa River between Osaka and Hyogo during the Bon Festival.
venue: Inagawa Riverbank (Kawanishi City, Hyogo Prefecture - Ikeda City, Osaka Prefecture)
access: Approximately a 10-minute walk from Hankyu Kawanishi Noseguchi Station or Hankyu Ikeda Station.
rain: Not announced.
